questions.text: How does the Surah use oaths (constellations, promised day) as a teaching method?
questions.answer: It points to cosmic signs and the promised Day to emphasize certainty of judgment.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that persecution proves believers are abandoned by Allah.
questions.answer: False. It shows trials happen, and Allah’s justice will prevail.

questions.text: What is the central lesson from the story of the People of the Trench?
questions.answer: Faith may be tested severely, but steadfast believers are honored and oppressors face Allah’s punishment.

questions.text: Why is the believers’ ‘crime’ described as simply believing in Allah?
questions.answer: To expose the ظلم of persecution and show that tawhid is often opposed by tyrants.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about repentance for oppressors?
questions.answer: The door of repentance exists, but refusing it leads to severe punishment.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests tyranny is safe if it is done publicly and confidently.
questions.answer: False. Allah witnesses everything and punishes injustice.

questions.text: How does describing Allah as 'severe in seizure' help a believer’s heart?
questions.answer: It restores confidence that ظلم will not last and that Allah can seize oppressors decisively.

questions.text: How does this Surah strengthen belief in resurrection?
questions.answer: It emphasizes Allah originates creation and repeats it, making resurrection certain.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Wadud' teach about Allah’s relationship with believers?
questions.answer: Allah loves and shows affection to His servants, especially those who remain faithful.

questions.text: How can belief in Allah’s love help during hardship?
questions.answer: It prevents despair and gives strength to endure trials with hope and dignity.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah’s will is limited by human power.
questions.answer: False. It teaches Allah does whatever He wills.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention Pharaoh and Thamud?
questions.answer: To remind that powerful tyrants were destroyed, so current oppressors are not beyond Allah’s reach.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah encompassing the disbelievers?
questions.answer: They are surrounded by Allah’s knowledge and power; they cannot escape accountability.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the Quran’s status and protection?
questions.answer: It is a glorious Quran preserved by Allah, showing revelation is protected from corruption.

questions.text: What is one lesson from the idea of a 'Preserved Tablet'?
questions.answer: Allah’s knowledge and decree are complete, and revelation is safeguarded.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that suffering automatically makes someone righteous.
questions.answer: False. It honors faithful patience; righteousness depends on faith and steadfastness.

questions.text: What is one modern application of this Surah for someone facing religious pressure?
questions.answer: Stay firm on tawhid, avoid compromise, and trust that Allah sees and will judge.

questions.text: How can a believer respond to ظلم without becoming unjust themselves?
questions.answer: Maintain patience, seek lawful means, make dua, and avoid ظلم in return.

questions.text: How does the Surah balance fear and hope?
questions.answer: It warns of Hell for persistent oppressors and promises Paradise as the great success for believers.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah’s justice may never appear.
questions.answer: False. It emphasizes the Promised Day and Allah’s certain justice.

questions.text: What are 'Al-Buruj'?
questions.answer: The Big Stars or the Constellations in the sky.

questions.text: What is the 'Promised Day' (Al-Yawm al-Maw'ud)?
questions.answer: The Day of Resurrection.

questions.text: Who are the 'Ashab al-Ukhdud'?
questions.answer: The 'Companions of the Trench' who persecuted the believers by throwing them into a fire-filled pit.

questions.text: What was the crime of the believers who were thrown into the trench?
questions.answer: They believed in Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy.

questions.text: Did the persecutors of the trench witness their own crime?
questions.answer: Yes, they sat by the fire and witnessed what they were doing to the believers.

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who persecute believing men and women and do not repent?
questions.answer: The punishment of Hell and the punishment of the Burning Fire.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Success' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Entering Gardens beneath which rivers flow.

questions.text: How is the 'Seizure' (Batsh) of Allah described?
questions.answer: As 'severe' (Shadid).

questions.text: Who originates creation and then repeats it?
questions.answer: Allah.

questions.text: Name three attributes of Allah mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: Al-Ghafur (The Forgiving), Al-Wadud (The Affectionate), and Lord of the Throne.

questions.text: What does 'Fa'alun lima yurid' mean?
questions.answer: Effecter of what He intends (He does whatever He wills).

questions.text: Which two powerful 'hosts' (armies) are mentioned as examples of those who were destroyed?
questions.answer: The hosts of Pharaoh and Thamud.

questions.text: In a 'Lawh-im-Mahfuz' (a Preserved Tablet).
questions.answer: In a 'Lawh-im-Mahfuz' (a Preserved Tablet).

questions.text: Is Allah a witness over all things?
questions.answer: Yes, Allah is Witness over all things.

questions.text: What is the main moral lesson of the story of the Trench?
questions.answer: Perseverance in faith despite extreme persecution, and the certainty of Allah's justice.

questions.text: What is 'Al-Wadud'?
questions.answer: The Most Loving or Most Affectionate.

questions.text: Does Allah encompass the disbelievers?
questions.answer: Yes, Allah encompasses them; no one escapes His knowledge and power.

questions.text: Why is the Quran called 'Majid'?
questions.answer: Because it is glorious and noble in its content and origin.

questions.text: What does 'the witness and the witnessed' (Shahidin wa Mashhud) refer to generally?
questions.answer: It refers to witnessing and being witnessed—an image of testimony and accountability before Allah.

questions.text: What happened to the people who sat around the trench?
questions.answer: They were cursed/destroyed for their cruelty.
